name: CSP: model connect-src as structured per-vendor groups
description: Altitude finding from the v1.18.0 release->main review (src/middleware.ts). The draft/non-draft connect-src branches duplicate every shared vendor origin (Sentry, R2, Mixpanel, Clarity, Bing); Clarity also appears across script-src/connect-src/img-src. Model the allowlist as structured per-vendor groups ({script,connect,img}) and compose each directive, with draft-only Sanity origins as one group. SCOPE: (a) per-buyer audio watermark on voice-note delivery (quiet, late in track, embeds submission ID hash); (b) per-buyer visible footer watermark on PDF delivery; (c) geo-reverify branch on /listen: if request IP geo-distance from purchase IP is large (>500km or country mismatch), render the email-verify form even if token is valid (Gumroad pattern per RESEARCH.md section 3); (d) token-redemption audit log for forwarding-detection signal (already partial via session cookie, formalize); (e) one-line 'this link is just for you' copy in delivery email body (could land in Phase 5 instead, doc here for completeness). name: Rich text editors for email body + signoff fields
description:
Becky asked for WYSIWYG editing of email body content (bold, italic, links, paragraph breaks). Currently each email singleton stores body content as plain string/text fields in studio/schemas/email*.ts. Decision: convert body + signoff fields (any multi-line/long-form surface) to Sanity Portable Text. Keep subject + button labels + short labels as simple strings.

Scope:
- studio/schemas/email{OrderConfirmation,Day7Delivery,MagicLink,GiftPurchaseConfirmation,GiftClaim,RecipientIntakeReceived,PrivacyExport}.ts — switch body + signOff field defs to array of [{type:'block'}]
- src/data/defaults.ts — convert string defaults to PortableTextBlock[] shape (1 block per paragraph)
- src/lib/emails/{OrderConfirmation,Day7Delivery,MagicLink,...}.tsx — render Portable Text via @portabletext/to-html or react-email-friendly equivalent; keep token slot rendering ({firstName}, {readingName}, etc.) working inside text spans
- src/lib/emails/slots.ts — token validator regex must still match across text-block traversal (token literals will appear as spans)
- New migration: scripts/migrate-email-bodies-to-portable-text-2026-05.ts — converts existing prod string content -> PortableText shape, idempotent (skip already-converted), additive setIfMissing pattern
- Vitest coverage for the new render path (token substitution + bold/italic preservation)

Acceptance:
- Becky can bold/italic/link text inside body + signoff fields in Studio
- Worker renders emails identically (HTML preserves formatting; tokens still substitute)
- Migration script applied to prod dataset; no editorial content lost

Effort: medium (~3-5h). Touches 7 schemas + 7 components + 1 migration.
result: Shipped via PR #182. 7 email schemas converted text→PortableText. PortableTextBody/Inline render path handles string|string[]|PT array; legacy defaults unchanged. 7/7 snapshot parity tests prove zero visual regression. Migration script ready for prod run.

name: Evaluate cross-client email testing tool (Litmus / Email-On-Acid / Resend preview)
description:
PLANNING / EVALUATION subtask. Locked 2026-05-24 by Max ("plan for it now" vs "defer until render bug").

WHY NEEDED
React Email + iframed preview shows Chrome rendering, not Gmail / Outlook / Apple Mail. Email clients strip CSS aggressively (no flexbox-gap, limited @media, no CSS custom properties without fallbacks in most). The iframe-pane preview catches edit-time copy/layout but NOT client-specific rendering bugs. Send-to-test (separate subtask) gives accurate Gmail rendering for Becky's inbox but doesn't cover Outlook desktop / Apple Mail dark mode / Yahoo / etc.

DELIVERABLE
Written eval report at www/MEMORY/WORK/<slug>/CROSS_CLIENT_EVAL.md comparing:

1. Litmus (litmus.com) — industry standard. Pricing tiers (free trial → Basic $99/mo → higher tiers). API + visual diff. 90+ client previews.
2. Email-On-Acid (emailonacid.com) — direct competitor to Litmus. Pricing. Coverage.
3. Resend's built-in preview — currently none in the dashboard; check 2026 state. Free if it exists.
4. Mailchimp's Inbox Inspector (if standalone access still exists).
5. HTMLemail.io / Email Comb minifiers — preprocessing tools that strip non-compatible CSS automatically; cheaper than visual tests, prevents some bugs before they ship.

DECISION CRITERIA
- Coverage breadth (how many clients tested per send).
- Cost vs project scale (~10 customers/month at launch).
- Integration shape: manual upload of HTML vs API webhook vs no-integration-needed (visual-diff a deployed-static-render).
- Whether dark-mode rendering is covered (Outlook + Apple Mail diverge here).

OUTPUT
Recommendation: adopt one + monthly budget, OR defer with explicit trigger (e.g., first customer reports render bug in client X). Then file the follow-up tasks if adopted.

NOT IN SCOPE
- Actual integration (separate subtask if eval recommends adoption).
- Free-tool patchwork as a substitute (e.g., manually opening each email in each client) — that's labor-intensive and doesn't scale; the eval is about tool-buy decision.

TRIGGER FOR EVAL: any time after the iframe-pane swap + send-to-test subtasks land. NOT blocking on launch.
result: Shipped via PR #225 commit 860fd58 on release/v1.5.0 as docs/CROSS_CLIENT_EMAIL_EVAL.md. Compared Litmus, Email-On-Acid, Resend native preview, Mailchimp Inbox Inspector, and CSS preprocessing tools. Decision: DEFER ADOPTION. At ~10 customers/month launch volume, $74-99/mo doesn't pencil. PR #226's send-to-test action covers Max's primary inbox. React Email's table-based render already conservative on CSS. Promotion trigger documented: 1+ customer render-bug report in 30-day window, OR 100+ customer emails/month, OR new template with non-trivial CSS, OR brand redesign. When trigger fires, pick Email-On-Acid Starter ~$74/mo over Litmus.

name: Research — Storybook 9.x for Next.js App Router + Tailwind v4 + Sanity + MCPs
description:
Deliverable: written research report at www/MEMORY/WORK/<slug>/RESEARCH.md with citations to vendor docs, blog posts, working examples in other projects. NOT a plan — the plan-discussion subtask reads this and locks decisions afterwards.

Topics:

1. STORYBOOK VERSION + NEXT.JS APP ROUTER + RSC
   - Latest stable Storybook (likely 9.x as of 2026); current LTS posture
   - @storybook/nextjs vs @storybook/experimental-nextjs-vite — which framework integration is recommended for App Router projects in 2026
   - RSC story support — what works, what's flagged experimental, edge cases
   - Server Action mocking, async-component stories, useFormState/useActionState handling

2. TAILWIND v4 + tokens.css WIRING
   - @theme directive in preview-head.html or .storybook/preview.ts
   - HMR for token changes (does editing tokens.css live-update stories?)
   - Compatibility with Storybook's PostCSS pipeline
   - Any known issues with Storybook + Tailwind v4 specifically (v4 is newer than most Storybook docs)

3. SANITY GROQ MOCKING
   - MSW handlers in browser context — workerd-isolate gotcha (CLAUDE.md known-pitfall) DOES NOT apply since Storybook runs in browser. Confirm.
   - Alternative: mock the Sanity client at module level via Storybook decorators or framework parameters
   - Alternative: feed fetched data as Story props (component takes already-fetched data; story bypasses fetch entirely)
   - Pattern: how to express the same page in different states (cold-visit / check-email / rested / DeliveredSurface for /listen) without hand-rolling 4 separate fetch mocks
   - Sanity Live (next-sanity defineLive) interaction with Storybook — does sanityFetch work in non-Next runtime?

4. NEXT.JS ROUTING MOCKING
   - useParams, useSearchParams, usePathname under @storybook/nextjs — supported via parameters.nextjs.navigation
   - notFound() / redirect() — how stories react
   - middleware.ts — does it run in Storybook context (it shouldn't)
   - Cookie-based auth (listen sessions) — best practice for storying cookie-gated states

5. STORYBOOK MCPs (the AI integration angle)
   - Confirm canonical MCP server (Storybook team announced one in 2025 — verify version, repo)
   - What it exposes (list stories, render a story, capture screenshot, get component props/args, etc.)
   - Whether the MCP can be used in Claude Code / this project's tooling
   - Alternative MCPs in the design-system / component-library space (Chromatic MCP? Tailwind MCP?) — quick survey

6. HOSTING / VISUAL REGRESSION
   - Local-only vs static export to Cloudflare Pages (existing CF account) vs Chromatic
   - If Chromatic: cost, free-tier limits, visual diff workflow
   - If static export: how to publish + access-gate (CF Access? Pages auth?)
   - CI integration: should PRs trigger story-build + visual diff? worth it at this project's scale?

7. WORKERD DEV-BINDINGS COMPATIBILITY
   - Does Storybook need access to D1 / R2 / DO bindings? (Should NOT — components consuming fetched data should be storied at the component layer, not via real bindings.)
   - Confirm @opennextjs/cloudflare imports don't error at story-load time
   - If they do: how to mock getCloudflareContext (it's already mocked in vitest per CLAUDE.md DO-bindings rule — pattern is known)

8. BUILD PERF + DEV-LOOP COST
   - How long does Storybook take to boot against this codebase?
   - HMR latency for a single component edit
   - Does running Storybook + pnpm dev simultaneously share build artifacts or duplicate work?

9. ALTERNATIVES (sanity check, not deep-dive)
   - Ladle, Histoire — quick comparison vs Storybook for this stack
   - Plain Next.js routes with mock data toggled via env (cheaper, less feature-rich)
   - Verdict: stay with Storybook unless something concrete disqualifies it

10. PRIOR-ART CHECK
    - Sanity + Next.js + Storybook example repos
    - Any existing patterns we can lift outright

Output: written report, not a plan. Plan-discussion subtask consumes this.
result: Report at www/MEMORY/WORK/20260523-203500_storybook-page-stories-research/RESEARCH.md. Headline: Storybook 10.3.6 + .storybook/ already shipped in repo with 15 component stories. Recommended migration: @storybook/nextjs → @storybook/nextjs-vite. Pages already 80% data-as-props (ListenView verified) — prop-injection stories with zero fetch mocking is the cheapest path. sb.mock pattern translates 1:1 from existing vitest @opennextjs/cloudflare mock. Storybook MCP shipped 10.3 (React-only). 4 open questions flagged for plan-discussion.

name: Replace native <select> in DatePicker + TimePicker with brand-styled custom dropdown
description:
Surfaced 2026-05-28 alongside the arrow-alignment task (sqnozgtu). DatePicker uses react-day-picker captionLayout="dropdown" which renders native <select> for month + year; TimePicker uses native <select> for hour + minute (src/components/Form/TimePicker/TimePicker.tsx:142, 160). When opened, both render the OS-native option list — bright white background, system font, sharp corners — which clashes visually with the cream + Cormorant + rounded-pill design language used everywhere else in the form (see screenshot 2026-05-28). Goal: option lists should match the booking form's design tokens (cream surface, Cormorant labels, j-rose hover, rounded corners, soft shadow).

Approach options:
  (a) Replace native <select> with Radix UI Select (or Headless UI Listbox) — fully stylable, a11y handled, keyboard nav works. Recommended.
  (b) Try CSS-only via appearance:none + custom arrow — does NOT restyle the dropdown panel itself (browsers won't let you), so only fixes the closed-state chrome, not the open option list. Won't satisfy the ask.
  (c) Hybrid — keep native for accessibility but accept the visual mismatch. Status-quo.

For DatePicker specifically: react-day-picker's captionLayout="dropdown" can be replaced by passing custom components via the components prop (MonthsDropdown, YearsDropdown) so the brand Select component renders in the caption slot.

Surfaces:
  - src/components/Form/DatePicker/DatePicker.tsx (captionLayout + classNames.dropdown)
  - src/components/Form/TimePicker/TimePicker.tsx:142, 160 (hour/minute selects)
  - Likely a new shared src/components/Form/Select/ brand-styled wrapper component if going Radix route.

Coordinate with sqnozgtu (arrow alignment) — both touch DatePicker caption row and should land together or one-after-the-other. Not launch-blocking — polish.
result: Radix Select primitive replaces native select in pickers; old Select renamed to FormSelect; shipped via PR #235

name: Mechanical comment-narration guard (vitest scanner)
description:
Recurrence-prevention for feedback_comments_over_logged. The rule is in memory + applied at session-load but it keeps getting violated across sessions and by dispatched Engineer agents. Memory alone isn't enough; need a mechanical guard that runs in CI.

Implementation: small vitest scanner in src/scanners/ following the SANDBOX_EMAIL_PREFIXES pattern (PR #214). Fails CI on:
- 'Phase \\d+' references in comments
- '\\bdex [a-z0-9]{6,}\\b' (dex task IDs)
- '\\bepic [a-z0-9]{6,}\\b'
- '\\b20\\d\\d-\\d{2}-\\d{2}\\b' inside comments (date prefixes)
- Multi-line comment blocks over 4 consecutive '//' lines in non-test source files (catches narration headers)

Allowlist: docs/, MEMORY/, *.test.*, *.md. Inline override marker for rare legitimate case (e.g., '// allow-narrate').

Covers: src/, scripts/, migrations/, studio/. Why mechanical: memory-only rules don't reliably gate code generation; this catches both my own drift and dispatched-agent output.

name: Env-gated email allowlist (belt-and-suspenders, layer 3)
description:
Belt-and-suspenders defense for the Resend leak that surfaced 2026-05-28 (PR #214 a5cfad1 plugged it via prefix list + scanner test; this is layer 3).

IN STAGING / SANDBOX environments only, reject ALL Resend sends to recipients NOT in a tiny PRODUCTION allowlist (Max, Becky, hello@withjosephine.com, the NOTIFICATION_EMAIL). Today the prefix-match guard depends on humans remembering to register new test prefixes. Layer 3 makes that drift IMPOSSIBLE: if env != production AND recipient not in production allowlist, fail-closed.

IMPLEMENTATION SKETCH:
- detect env via NEXT_PUBLIC_SANITY_DATASET !== 'production' OR equivalent worker-env signal
- new helper isProductionAllowlistedRecipient(addr): true ONLY for an allowlist of ~5 production identities
- in resolveSkipReason: if env != production && !isProductionAllowlistedRecipient(recipient) -> return 'env_guard' SkipReason
- log loudly when this guard fires (would be a regression-detection signal)

WHY: Max said 2026-05-28 'resend / emails shouldn’t work in any testing'. Layers 1 (prefix list) + 2 (scanner test) catch ~all sandbox-spec drift, but they can’t catch: (a) someone running a script locally against staging env that calls into the send path; (b) future surface that introduces new email-sending paths from cron/DO that don’t go through sendOrSkip; (c) the FROM line accidentally including a real recipient.

SCOPE: src/lib/resend.tsx + src/lib/resend.test.ts updates. ~30 LoC.

PRIORITY: p1 (production-risk class, but not blocking ship as long as layers 1+2 hold).

COORDINATION: see [[feedback_no_emails_in_testing_period]] + [[feedback_resend_dry_run_paths]].

name: Hosted Studio send-preview fails cross-origin (Failed to fetch)
description: On withjosephine.sanity.studio the send-email-preview action fetches the RELATIVE /api/admin/list-preview-recipients (and /send-email-preview). On the sanity.studio host this redirects to www.sanity.io/@.../studio/... and is CORS-blocked (Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://admin.sanity.io != studio origin) — never reaches the Worker. Pre-existing since v1.5.0 (#227); surfaced now because the Studio was un-deployable from v1.7.0 until the 2026-06-18 pickDefined alias fix, and #297 made the failing fetch fire on dialog-open. No embedded /studio route on the Worker; the action has no origin resolution; admin routes send no CORS headers. FIX: point the action at the Worker origin (per-workspace previewOrigin already in sanity.config.ts sharedPlugins) + add CORS to both admin routes — Access-Control-Allow-Origin for the sanity.studio origin on the GET, plus an OPTIONS preflight for the JSON POST. Needs Worker redeploy (via release->staging) + Studio redeploy. SECURITY: these routes are token-less since #297, so opening CORS to the public Studio origin widens them to any caller from that origin, bounded only by ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS — make that call deliberately (alt: restore lightweight auth, see the #297 reconsideration). name: Automate 3-year reading-content deletion
description:
Privacy policy declares reading content deleted after 3 years (READING_CONTENT_RETENTION_YEARS=3) but nothing enforces it. cleanup cron only handles unpaid intake (30d) + R2 photo (90d); paid Sanity submissions (intake answers, voice note, PDF assets) persist until manual Studio cascade. Plan (reuse, do not rebuild):

2. Reuse content-deletion half of cascadeDeleteUser / deleteCustomerData: delete Sanity submission (voiceNote+readingPdf assets + responses[]) + residual R2 photo; EXPLICITLY skip financial_records (6yr HMRC, migration 0005 documents the asymmetry).
3. Do NOT fire Stripe/Brevo/Mixpanel redaction — this is retention-expiry, not Art.17 erasure. Confirm scoping with privacy counsel.
4. Summary log + idempotency guard.
5. Test: >3yr paid submission content deleted while financial_records row survives; staging e2e against back-dated fixture.

name: my-readings: Readings tab unreachable for gift-only accounts (defaultTab=gifts no-op)
description: Smoke 2026-05-27 staging, maxgertzen@gmail.com (0 readings, 3 gifts). Clicking the Readings tab does nothing. Root cause src/app/my-readings/_shared/LibraryView.tsx:104 'const activeTab = pathname === GIFTS_PATH ? gifts : defaultTab'. When readings empty + gifts present, pickDefaultTab returns 'gifts', so at READINGS_PATH (/my-readings) activeTab=defaultTab='gifts'. handleChange (line 106-112) only router.push-es when nextPath !== pathname; selecting 'readings' targets /my-readings which already === pathname, so push is skipped (no-op). And activeTab has no client state to hold the explicit selection, so it always recomputes to defaultTab. Net: gift-only purchasers can never open the (empty) Readings tab. Masked when user has >=1 reading (defaultTab=readings, paths differ). Fix options: (a) when defaultTab=gifts redirect /my-readings -> /my-readings/gifts so path is the single source of truth and Readings click navigates cleanly; (b) hold activeTab in client useState initialized from defaultTab so handleChange updates state even when path is unchanged. Needs a test for the gift-only case. Phase 2 surface.
result: Closed at the root by PR #208 (dex 0bjxc38w): removing the tab strip eliminates the unreachable-tab path entirely. Both sections now render unconditionally for gift-only accounts.

name: Cloudflare dashboard: allow AI assistant crawlers on prod
description: Prod (withjosephine.com) returns edge 403 to all AI crawler UAs via CF AI bot blocking; verified 2026-07-06 (browser UA 200, ClaudeBot/Claude-User/Claude-SearchBot 403, server: cloudflare, no worker headers). Max decided: allow Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity assistant/search bots; keep training opt-out (robots.ts unchanged, Google-Extended stays disallowed so Gemini grounding remains robots-opted-out). Dashboard-only, no API for per-crawler actions. Steps: (1) dash.cloudflare.com -> withjosephine.com -> AI Crawl Control -> Crawlers tab -> Allow: Anthropic (Claude-User, Claude-SearchBot, ClaudeBot*), OpenAI (ChatGPT-User, OAI-SearchBot), Google, Perplexity (PerplexityBot, Perplexity-User); leave Bytespider/CCBot/meta-externalagent etc. blocked. *ClaudeBot at CF layer optional; robots.ts still disallows it. (2) Security -> Bots -> Configure Bot Fight Mode -> turn OFF 'Instruct bot traffic with robots.txt' (new UI: Security Settings -> Bot traffic filter -> block-training-in-robots.txt toggle) so the CF-managed robots.txt section (ai-train=no + Disallow blocks) stops overriding app robots.ts. (3) Verify: curl -A 'Claude-User/1.0' https://withjosephine.com/ expects 200; robots.txt no longer contains 'BEGIN Cloudflare Managed content'.
result: Done 2026-07-06 with Max driving the dashboard. Actual mechanism differed from the plan: the blocker was AI Crawl Control -> Security -> Settings -> 'Block AI bots Scope' (Cloudflare-managed rule, was 'Block on all pages') which overrode per-crawler row toggles; set to 'Do not block', then per-row Block toggles applied. Verified live: assistant/search bots 200 (Claude-User via real Anthropic-infra WebFetch renders full site; Claude-SearchBot, ChatGPT-User, OAI-SearchBot, PerplexityBot, Perplexity-User via curl), training/scrapers 403 (ClaudeBot, GPTBot, Bytespider, CCBot, Amazonbot, meta-externalagent). Managed robots.txt left ON deliberately (its Disallow list matches the keep-training-blocked policy; does not touch assistant bots). robots.ts unchanged; Google-Extended stays disallowed so Gemini grounding remains robots-opted-out by choice. Caveat learned: spoofed-UA curls from a non-vendor IP are unreliable ground truth while managed rules are active; real-infra fetch or dashboard Allowed-counters are the proof.

name: 90-day reading access policy + R2 lifecycle (retention parity)
description:
Today: listen-page HMAC tokens default to 1-year expiry (LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET config); CheckEmailCard re-issues indefinitely; R2 voice-note + PDF objects have NO lifecycle rule. Net: customer access = forever, data retention = forever. GDPR Art. 5(1)(e) data minimization exposure.

New policy (locked 2026-05-24 with Max): **90-day access window per reading**, then no self-service. After 90d: customer must email Josephine for manual re-issue (admin route already exists at /api/internal/issue-magic-link).

Scope:
- src/lib/auth/listenSession.ts (or wherever LISTEN_TOKEN expiry is set at mint time) — change default from 1y -> 90d
- src/lib/auth/users.ts magic-link mint path -> 90d expiresAt
- src/components/CheckEmailCard/ -> hide or 404 when submission deliveredAt + 90d < now; show 'expired — email hello@withjosephine.com' affordance
- src/app/my-readings/page.tsx — each reading row past 90d: replace 'Open' CTA with 'Expired — email hello@withjosephine.com' (link to mailto with subject prefilled including readingId)
- src/lib/emails/Day7Delivery.tsx + EMAIL_DAY7_DELIVERY_DEFAULTS — copy update to mention 90d window: 'Your link is good for the next 90 days. After that, just email me and I'll send a fresh one.'
- R2 lifecycle: configure rule on the voice-note + PDF prefix(es) in the prod bucket (withjosephine-booking-photos? or a separate readings bucket — verify). Expire objects 120d after upload (90d access + 30d grace for slow-listening customers + admin re-issue window before deletion).
- Confirm /api/internal/issue-magic-link still works as the admin escape hatch (already exists per grep)
- Privacy policy + Terms text update — define the 90d retention window explicitly
- Vitest: token mint with 90d expiry; CheckEmailCard expired branch; /my-readings expired-row branch
- E2e spec: customer with delivered_at older than 90d can't re-issue self-service; admin can

R2 audit needed before implementation:
- Where do voice-note + PDF actually live? withjosephine-booking-photos? josephine-backups? Or Sanity Assets? (booking-photos shows 0 objects in your screenshot; staging has 3 in withjosephine-booking-photos-staging)
- Confirm key prefix structure so lifecycle rule scopes correctly (don't accidentally expire intake photos)

Acceptance:
- New reading delivered today -> listen-page HMAC URL works for 90d, then 404s
- Day-7 email copy mentions 90d window
- CheckEmailCard self-service disappears past 90d, replaced with email-Josephine CTA
- /my-readings rows past 90d show expired state with mailto
- Admin route can mint fresh link for stale readings
- R2 lifecycle rule deletes voice-note + PDF objects ~120d after upload
- Privacy policy text updated to declare 90d retention

Effort: high (~6-9h). Touches auth mint, UI affordances, email copy, R2 infra, legal docs. Worth its own sub-PR.
result: Shipped via PR #184. Code-side 90d retention gate on listen + /my-readings + Day-7 email copy. R2 lifecycle + privacy/terms wording deferred as Max-actions in PR body.

name: Studio desk restructure — Pages / Emails / Form Building Blocks
description:
Restructure www/studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts into three top-level groups, separating singletons (configured surfaces) from document-type collections (reusable atoms):

📄 Pages (every customer-facing browser surface, comprehensive):
  - Landing, Under Construction, 404 (currently at root)
  - Listen, MyReadings, MyGifts, Magic Link Verify, Gift Claim, Gift Intake (currently in 'Customer emails & pages')
  - 📋 Booking Flow (nested S.list inside Pages):
      - Booking Page, Thank You Page (singletons)
      - Booking Form, Booking Gift Form (singletons — the configured forms)

✉️ Emails (currently interleaved with pages — break out as peer of Pages):
  - emailOrderConfirmation, emailGiftPurchaseConfirmation, emailGiftClaim, emailRecipientIntakeReceived, emailDay2Started, emailDay7Delivery, emailMagicLink, emailPrivacyExport
  - Preserve emailSingletonListItem helper (S.view.component(EmailPreview))

🧩 Form Building Blocks (document-type collections, the reusable atoms):
  - Form Fields (S.documentTypeListItem)
  - Form Sections (S.documentTypeListItem)

Root unchanged: Site Settings, Theme · Readings, Testimonials, FAQ Items · Legal Pages · Submissions

Rationale: Distinguishes singletons (one-doc-per-type configured surfaces) from document-type collections (multi-doc atoms). Pages is comprehensive AND honest (no schema atoms mislabeled as pages). Booking Flow keeps the two form singletons clustered with their Booking Page so the 'new reading type' workflow stays cohesive. Form Fields/Sections rarely accessed directly — Sanity lets editors create them inline via reference picker from inside Booking Form; the Form Building Blocks group is a management view, not primary edit surface.

BLOCKED on mc6p8m8t (Phase 7 email-in-Studio) — that epic owns deskStructure.ts via the EmailPreview wiring; touching the same file now = merge conflict. Start once Phase 7 PR merges.

Risk: zero data risk (singleton documentIds unchanged, only list-item arrangement changes).
result: Bundled into release/v1.2.0 via PR #175 (squash 42d5ac2). 3 top-level groups: Pages (9 surfaces + nested Booking Flow), Emails (8 singletons with Preview tab), Form Building Blocks (Form Fields + Form Sections). name: Plumb customer-name tokens into emailMagicLink (refactor to vars+applyTokens pattern)
description:
Allow Becky to use customer-name + magic-link tokens in any field of the magic-link email.

CURRENT STATE
1. UserRecord = { id, email } only — no firstName on the user record. Magic link is sent to anyone trying to authenticate, including strangers who don't have a reading yet.
2. MagicLink React component takes destructured props (magicLinkUrl, preview, greeting, body, signOff), NOT the vars+copy pattern. applyTokens isn't wired in. So even {magicLinkUrl} doesn't substitute in body copy today.

PLUMBING REQUIRED
1. Refactor MagicLink.tsx to vars+copy pattern, call applyTokens(rawCopy, vars) at top, drop the destructured props.
2. Update sendMagicLink in resend.tsx to pass vars { magicLinkUrl } (and later firstName/readingName/readingPriceDisplay).
3. Look up user's most-recent paid submission (if any) at sendMagicLink call sites:
   - src/app/api/auth/magic-link/route.ts (POST /api/auth/magic-link)
   - src/app/api/internal/issue-magic-link/route.ts (admin path)
4. Pass firstName + readingName + readingPriceDisplay into sendMagicLink args (with 'there' / '' fallbacks for users with no submission).
5. Widen emailMagicLink slot allowlist in src/lib/emails/slots.ts (currently []) to include magicLinkUrl + firstName + readingName + readingPriceDisplay.
6. Update src/lib/emails/preview-fixtures.ts so Preview tab shows substituted tokens.
7. Update src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx MagicLink render branch.
8. Tests: src/lib/resend.test.ts (sendMagicLink suite) — assert tokens substitute; add cases for unknown-user fallback.

WHY DEFERRED FROM PR #177
Two engineering scopes:
- The user-context join + fallback handling for stranger-with-no-submission cases
- MagicLink component refactor to vars+applyTokens pattern
Both filed for a separate session.
result: Shipped via PR #224 commit b83b4c7 on release/v1.5.0. MagicLink.tsx refactored to vars+copy applyTokens pattern. emailMagicLink allowlist widened to [magicLinkUrl, firstName, readingName, readingPriceDisplay]; emailMagicLinkLibrary to [magicLinkUrl, firstName]. New src/lib/auth/magicLinkVars.ts helper looks up most-recent paid submission via the new findMostRecentPaidByRecipientUserId repo method (added in the simplify polish commit 27c65aa). MAGIC_LINK_VARS_FALLBACK shape returned when user has no paid submission. Subject goes through applyTokens. extractFirstName promoted to exported helper. Wired into /api/auth/magic-link route. 2096/2096 vitest.

name: Send-to-test doc action (env-var recipient allowlist)
description:
ADDITIVE to the iframe-pane swap — gives Becky a real-world cross-client render check by sending the rendered email to a pre-approved inbox.

DECISION (locked 2026-05-24 by Max): recipient allowlist lives as an env var on the Worker (ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS comma-separated), NOT in Sanity siteSettings. Rationale: Studio compromise can't widen the allowlist; rotating recipients = deploy. Faster + safer security boundary.

WHAT
1. Studio doc-action: studio/actions/sendEmailPreview.ts. Available on each of the 8 email singletons. Title: "Send preview to inbox…". Disabled if doc has unpublished changes (force publish first to preview the published copy; current draft preview already lives in the iframe).
2. Action UI: <Dialog> with a select dropdown of allowlisted recipients (fetched from a new worker endpoint that returns the env-var list — never expose the raw env var to Studio bundle).
3. New worker endpoint: POST /api/admin/send-email-preview. Body: { type, recipient }. Auth: ADMIN_API_KEY header (same pattern as existing studio/actions/resendCustomerEmail.ts). Validates recipient is in ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS env-var before send. Re-uses src/lib/resend.tsx send functions with the current published Sanity content for that type (NOT defaults — Becky is previewing her edited copy).
4. New worker endpoint: GET /api/admin/list-preview-recipients (auth: ADMIN_API_KEY). Returns the allowlist for the dropdown.
5. Audit row written to deletion_log-equivalent (or a new admin_actions log) per send: { actor: "studio-admin", action: "preview_send", emailType, recipient, sentAt }. Same shape as cascadeDeleteUser audit.
6. Rate-limit: re-use the existing WAF rate-limit on /api/admin/* (rl-josephine-api-write-paths covers it implicitly once the path matches) OR add an in-worker per-recipient cooldown (3 sends per recipient per email-type per 24h, mirroring the existing resend-customer-email cooldown).

PRECEDENT
- studio/actions/resendCustomerEmail.ts (existing — published 2026-05-18 in v1.1.0) is the closest pattern. Lift its shape (auth header, audit write, cooldown).
- See www/MEMORY/WORK/20260517-220000_phase-7-email-sanity-cms/PRD.md for the D-4 origin/referer auth posture (this endpoint can use the stronger ADMIN_API_KEY since it's not iframe-loaded).

DOES NOT BLOCK iframe-pane swap or seed migration — strictly additive. Can ship in PR-A1 if scope allows, OR as a follow-up PR-A3.
result: Shipped via PR #226 commit 0ed9e77 on release/v1.5.0. Studio 'Send preview to inbox...' doc action on 12 customer email singletons. New admin routes: GET /api/admin/list-preview-recipients + POST /api/admin/send-email-preview (x-admin-token auth, REFUSED(404) on auth fail). ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS env var documented in wrangler.jsonc; unset = 503 + dialog shows 'not configured' (fail-closed). New audit event admin_email_preview_sent. New EmailSubType admin_email_preview. Subject prefixed with [PREVIEW] so test renders to shared inbox cannot be mistaken for real customer emails. Per the simplify polish commit 27c65aa, sendEmailPreview routes through canonical sendOrSkip (sandbox-prefix guard, RESEND_DRY_RUN, x-e2e-resend-dry-run header, cached client, redacted logging, serverTrack). Per-recipient cooldown deferred — WAF rate-limit (5 req/10s/IP) + admin-token + env-allowlist combination is sufficient defense-in-depth at v1.5.0 volume. MAX-ACTION POST-MERGE: set ALLOWED_PREVIEW_RECIPIENTS worker secret on staging + prod (value: hello@withjosephine.com,maxgertzen+withjosephine-preview@gmail.com) per SESSION_BOOT.

name: Sanity validation harness — catch schema-vs-data drift locally + CI
description:
Max-flagged 2026-05-24 after Day-7 email schema validation errors surfaced in production Studio. Migration script existed (scripts/migrate-email-bodies-to-portable-text-2026-05.mts) but wasn't required to run pre-deploy, so production data drifted from schema. Required two manual migration runs this session (staging + production) AFTER Max saw the validation banner in Studio.

==== BUILD SCOPE ====

(1) Script: pnpm sanity:validate <staging|production>
- Connects to Sanity via SANITY_READ_TOKEN
- Walks each known singleton (10 customer-facing emails + pages per scripts/seed-customer-emails-and-pages.mts)
- For each field in each schema, asserts actual value type matches schema expected type
- Specifically catches: type 'text' (string) where schema expects array of block (PortableText)
- Exit code non-zero on drift + per-field summary report

(2) Wire (1) into CI as POST-DEPLOY check that fails the release-branch CI if deployed worker code expects shapes the dataset doesn't supply. Add as a job in .github/workflows/ci.yml after the deploy-staging job.

(3) For schema-touching PRs: REQUIRE the migration script be runnable + dry-run-mode verified. Add to PR template or to sentry-skills:pr-writer description guidance.

(4) Inverse check: schema deprecations should not leave orphan data on existing docs. Specifically catch the zombie 'consent' field type in studio/schemas/formField.ts noted in CLAUDE.md known pitfalls section.

==== CONTEXT FROM TONIGHT ====

The validation drift compounded the email-preview blank debugging. Schema expected Array, doc held String, Sanity Studio displayed validation banners for every doc, Max thought it was a render bug, then we found the migration step had been skipped on prod. Both problems would have been caught by an automated pre-publish/post-deploy validation pass.
result: Shipped via commit 345e6fa on release/v1.2.1. scripts/sanity-validate.mts + sibling contract module + CI post-deploy job (sanity-validate-staging, needs deploy-staging). Three severity categories: DRIFT (fails CI), MISSING (warning), WARNING (zombie consent probe). Final staging run: drift=0 missing=2 warnings=0.

Pattern: <BrandHeader shell={shell} /> + <GoldHero text={heroLine} /> + <EmailFooter shell={shell} />. Each template collapses ~30 lines of inline JSX to 3 component calls. name: Preview iframe — image rendering limitation (CORP same-origin)
description:
Tracked from PR #198 (kf1nixad PR-2): the next/image stub currently replaces all relative-path img srcs with a transparent SVG data URL because withjosephine.com sends Cross-Origin-Resource-Policy: same-origin on /images/* responses, which blocks the sandbox-iframe (null origin) preview from loading the actual images.

Three viable directions when this becomes painful enough:
1. Inline a small set of brand-critical images as base64/SVG data URLs in studio/preview-stubs/ — preview shows real logo, no production change.
2. Mirror /public/images into the Studio bundle under studio/.generated/images/ via a prebuild script, serve them at the iframe-host origin (Studio's own origin) so same-origin holds. More machinery.
3. Relax CORP on /images/* responses in middleware or static-asset handler (risk: weakens prod isolation; needs a real-world threat model first).

Trigger: Becky asks to see the rendered image preview, OR a future preview surface depends on inline imagery for editorial clarity (e.g. brand-decorative SVGs).

Current state: Footer logo + any /public/images/* asset renders as transparent placeholder; layout preserved exactly via width/height/className.

Reference: studio/preview-stubs/next-image.tsx (TRANSPARENT_PIXEL constant + resolveSrc logic).
result: Auto-closed by PR #261 merge (2c36e17f)

name: Migrate hand-rolled picker popovers to a battle-tested lib (Radix / Floating UI)
description: FOLLOW-UP from the picker stacking arc (PR #210 z-30-wrapper-lift then PR #211 portal-rewrite). DatePicker, TimePicker, PlaceAutocomplete each carry a hand-rolled usePopoverPortal hook (open/close + click-outside + bounding-rect position + window scroll/resize listeners + createPortal to document.body). This class of bug (ancestor stacking trap, click-outside edge cases, scroll/resize misalignment, focus management, ESC handling, a11y attributes) is exactly what popover libs solve once. Migrate to @radix-ui/react-popover (preferred — owns Portal + Trigger + Content + a11y + focus + ESC + click-outside out of the box) OR @floating-ui/react (more granular if Radix overrides are too opinionated). Acceptance: usePopoverPortal hook removed; DatePicker/TimePicker/PlaceAutocomplete use the lib's Popover primitives; visual + behavior parity verified at 375px and desktop; 21 picker tests still pass. Refs lc9w5xd1, PR #210, PR #211.
result: Migrated DatePicker, TimePicker, PlaceAutocomplete to @radix-ui/react-popover via PR #212 (squash 28554a6). Deleted hand-rolled usePopoverPortal hook. Radix owns Portal + Floating UI positioning + collision detection + a11y + focus + ESC + click-outside. This task was filed as a post-launch follow-up but shipped immediately to resolve the lc9w5xd1 stacking bug after PR #210 (z-30 wrapper) and PR #211 (manual createPortal) both left visible bleed-through.

name: MagicLink — add visible-URL fallback line below the button
description: Code-review MEDIUM finding from 2026-05-25 PR #193: refactor removed the visible-URL fallback from MagicLink.tsx. Old template rendered <Link>{magicLinkUrl}</Link> as clickable + visible body text. New template only exposes the URL via the styled button's href. If a recipient's email client strips the button styling (Outlook Word renderer with no border-radius, dark-mode masking the ink background), they may see only the unstyled label with no way to copy the URL. Auth-recovery is high-stakes; locked-out users = real support load. Fix: add a small 12-13px muted text line below the button: 'Or copy this link: <Link>{magicLinkUrl}</Link>'. Tests should assert the URL appears as both href AND body text. Affects all 3 magic-link variants (one component).
result:
Shipped via PR #195 squash-merged into release/v1.2.1.

Added a 13px muted "Or copy this link: <URL>" line directly below the magic-link button in MagicLink.tsx. The URL now renders as both the button href AND visible body text, so recipients on hostile clients (Outlook Word renderer, dark-mode masking the ink background, anti-spam button rewriters) can still copy and paste their sign-in link.

All three magic-link variants share this single component (emailMagicLink, emailMagicLinkMyReadings, emailMagicLinkMyGifts), so one change covers every magic-link send. New test asserts URL appears in both linkHrefs and visibleText. Closes the code-review MEDIUM finding from PR #193 review.

commit.message: fix(emails): Render magic-link URL as visible text below the button (#195)

name: Gated-page preview replan — Studio-bundled (supersedes sub-PR 5)
description:
PRIORITY (post vej2v0x5). Gated-page preview replan, Studio-bundled. Becky needs to see the page she's editing in Studio. PR #185's /preview/* worker routes broke because CF Access on staging challenges the iframe.

USE THE PROVEN PATTERN from vej2v0x5/EmailPreview.tsx:
- Studio-bundled component import (allowlist path in sanity.cli.ts STUDIO_SRC_ALIASES)
- Render to HTML string with @react-email/render or react-dom/server in browser
- iframe with srcDoc + sandbox='' + key={html ? 'loaded' : 'empty'} (the Chromium no-renavigate fix)

SCOPE — Phase 1 (this sub-PR):
1. Extract presentational layer from src/app/listen/[id]/ListenView.tsx, src/app/my-readings/MyReadingsView.tsx, src/app/my-gifts/MyGiftsView.tsx — strip server-only deps (Sanity Live live-queries, cookies(), headers(), DB calls). Pure-props variants.
2. Allowlist new aliases in sanity.cli.ts (e.g. @/components/listen, etc.)
3. Tailwind v4 → Studio bundle via @tailwindcss/vite plugin in sanity.cli.ts vite extension. Extract src/app/globals.css token + utilities, inline as <style> tag in the srcDoc HTML.
4. Build StudioPagePreview.tsx mirroring EmailPreview.tsx:
   - Sanity UI Select for state picker (replaces URL ?state= from PR #185)
   - renderToString or react-email render → HTML
   - Inline Tailwind CSS + font links
   - iframe srcDoc + sandbox + key prop (the proven recipe)
5. Replace existing broken studio/views/PagePreview.tsx (PR #185 version).

DECISION NEEDED FROM MAX: revert /preview/<surface> worker routes from PR #185, or keep them as standalone-browser bookmark fallback (work in incognito with manual CF Access bypass).

RISKS:
- Tailwind collides with @sanity/ui's styles — class precedence + bundle size
- View components likely import server-only modules transitively — careful path surgery needed
- Studio bundle size grows (Tailwind CSS + View components)

PHASE 2 (separate task — to be filed): scale to public pages if any of them aren't already covered by Sanity Presentation Tool. Today's public pages (Home, BookingForm, Thank-You, etc.) iframe the live withjosephine.com URL via Presentation Tool — should already work. VERIFY before filing Phase 2.

name: [Phase 3] Step-up OTP auth (elevatedAt session field)
description: GOAL: introduce defense-in-depth step-up auth for high-risk mutations on /my-gifts (tab inside /my-readings post Phase 2). Required because the listen-session cookie is session-bound (authorizeGiftPurchaser at src/app/api/gifts/[id]/_lib/authorizeGiftPurchaser.ts:57) and once stolen all mutations are open today. SCOPE: (a) add elevatedAt timestamp column to the session/auth row (or co-located store); (b) build OTP email-verify flow: short 6-digit code mailed to purchaserEmail, validated on a step-up endpoint, sets elevatedAt = now; (c) gate the following 3 mutation endpoints on elevatedAt > now - 10min: /api/gifts/[id]/edit-recipient, /api/gifts/[id]/send-now, the new claim-for-yourself endpoint from Phase 4; (d) UI: when an elevated mutation is attempted without elevation, show a one-line message ('we will email you a code to confirm, protects against forwarded-link misuse') + OTP entry; (e) elevation TTL 10 minutes so a purchaser editing 4 things does not get 4 emails; (f) failed OTP (wrong/expired) routes to contact form per Priya's support-load mitigation, not a dead-end Unauthorized. ACCEPTANCE: edit-recipient + send-now + claim-for-yourself all require fresh OTP per 10-min window; date/message/resend-to-same/status-view are unchanged (no step-up). NOT IN SCOPE: passkeys (deferred to future epic per Phase 2 of council). DEPENDS ON: Phase 2 (library page consolidation provides the surface). Council ref: COUNCIL-2-library-auth.md recommendation #1, Devi technical implementation note.
result: Phase 3 step-up OTP shipped on release/v1.4.0 via PR #207 (squash 9238f82). Migration 0016 applied to staging D1. Staging worker deployed with Phase 2 + Phase 3 (deploy-staging job ID 26457695131). Full surface: D1 elevated_at column + step_up_otp ledger, HKDF stepup.v1 subkey, OTP primitive with 15-min code + 10-min elevation + 5-attempt poison + 30s/3-per-30min throttle, request/verify endpoints, requireElevation guard on edit-recipient + send-now, Sanity emailStepUpOtp + StepUpOtp.tsx, StepUpOtpModal with full a11y. 76/76 ISC. 2054/2054 vitest tests passing. name: Josephine — edit + preview emails in Studio (Phase 7, paused on branch)
description:
WHAT IT DOES (the feature Josephine sees in Sanity Studio):

Josephine opens any of the 7 customer-facing email singletons in Studio and edits subject / preview / greeting / body / CTA / signoff as plain Sanity fields. The document pane carries a second tab `Preview` (S.view.component(EmailPreview)) that renders a sandboxed iframe of the rendered React Email — live, against draft content (Sanity `previewDrafts` perspective). So she edits → tabs to Preview → sees the email as a customer would, before publishing. Slot validation (`{firstName}`, `{readingName}`, etc.) blocks publish on any unknown token, so a typo can't ship as literal text into a customer inbox.

COVERED TEMPLATES (7):
- emailOrderConfirmation
- emailDay2Started
- emailDay7Delivery
- emailGiftClaim
- emailGiftPurchaseConfirmation
- emailMagicLink
- emailPrivacyExport (new singleton in this branch — replaces the inline-HTML GDPR Art. 20 export)

- src/lib/emails/slots.ts (+ tests) — canonical EMAIL_ALLOWED_SLOTS + extract/validate/format helpers
- src/lib/emails/PrivacyExport.tsx (+ tests) — React Email template for Art. 20
- src/lib/emails/preview-fixtures.ts — fixture vars + per-template defaults map
- src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx — iframe content endpoint (Origin/Referer allowlist + type validation + draft fetch via SANITY_READ_TOKEN + previewDrafts perspective + dispatch across all 7 templates)
- studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx — Sanity document-pane view (sandboxed iframe pointed at preview route)
- studio/lib/validateSlots.ts — Sanity Rule.custom helper importing the canonical allowlist
- studio/schemas/emailPrivacyExport.ts — new singleton
- studio/schemas/emailOrderConfirmation/Day2/Day7/GiftClaim/GiftPurchase/MagicLink — slot-validation wired on every slot-bearing field
- studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts — emailSingletonListItem helper attaching .views([form, Preview])
- src/middleware.ts — isEmailPreview path-gate for /api/email-preview/*

name: Pivot email previews to in-component Studio render
description:
Current state: Studio iframe points at worker route /api/email-preview/[type], which fetches Sanity draft via @sanity/preview-url-secret then renders via @react-email/render and returns HTML. Two failure modes hit in v1.2.x: (a) middleware short-circuits all /api/* to under-construction page while apex is parked (smoke + preview broken on prod); (b) STUDIO_ORIGIN_ALLOWLIST CSP frame-ancestors needs hardcoded prod studio URL.

Pivot: render the same React Email components directly in the Studio iframe via @react-email/render in the browser. No worker round-trip, no auth dance, no CSP exception, no middleware exposure.

Scope:
- Audit src/lib/emails/{OrderConfirmation,Day7Delivery,MagicLink,GiftPurchaseConfirmation,GiftClaim,RecipientIntakeReceived,PrivacyExport}.tsx for worker-only imports (env reads, Resend SDK, server-only modules). Remove or factor.
- Expose templates from a shared module (e.g. src/lib/emails/index.ts or lift to a package boundary studio can import without pulling in worker bindings)
- Rewrite studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx to: import the matching template, fetch fixture vars from src/lib/emails/preview-fixtures.ts, render with editorDoc state + PREVIEW_DEFAULTS merge, output via @react-email/render in-browser, set srcdoc on iframe
- Delete src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx
- Delete @sanity/preview-url-secret usage (the validatePreviewUrl + createPreviewSecret dance)
- Remove STUDIO_ORIGIN_ALLOWLIST + STUDIO_LOCAL_DEV_ORIGINS env vars from worker config (no longer needed for this path)
- Remove the email-preview CSP frame-ancestors carve-out from middleware
- Pentester re-audit on the new path (preview now renders client-side with Studio-session content; confirm no PII bleed)

Acceptance:
- Becky can preview any of 8 customer emails from production Studio without 404/CSP/auth errors
- Preview refreshes on draft save without round-trip latency
- HTML output identical to current /api/email-preview route output
- Worker bundle size shrinks (worker no longer imports @sanity/preview-url-secret + @react-email/render for the preview path)

Effort: medium-high (~5-8h). Bulk is verifying templates are pure-functional and Studio's bundle config can import them without worker tangle.
result: Shipped via PR #181 (squash 950485f). Studio email preview now renders client-side; worker route + preview-secret + STUDIO_ORIGIN_ALLOWLIST all removed. Pentester clean; one [LOW] alias-narrowing fix folded in.

name: Plan: heuristic meta-retro — generalize learnings across projects/sessions
description:
Planning task (no execution yet — design the approach first).

Goal: take the raw retrospective output from the parent task and run a *heuristic* pass over it — i.e. not just 'what happened on Josephine' but 'what patterns repeat across sessions/projects and what can we change in the work process itself.'

Inputs:
- SessionHarvester output across this workspace (parent task surfaces it)
- Optionally: SessionHarvester across other project workspaces (~/dev/projects/*) to triangulate
- Existing global feedback memories (~/.claude/projects/*/memory/feedback_*.md)
- ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md AI Steering Rules — candidate edits land here, not in project CLAUDE.md

Heuristics to apply:
- Which mistakes/frictions repeat across >1 project? (those become global steering rules, not project-local)
- Which decisions, once made, kept getting relitigated? (those become binding-constraint patterns)
- Which workflows produced euphoric surprise vs grinding? (efficiency signal)
- Where did subagent delegation help vs hurt? (process tuning)
- Which memory entries went stale fast? (memory hygiene)

Output of THIS planning task: a short plan doc describing the heuristic method, the output schema (e.g. 'global rules to add', 'project-local rules to add', 'process changes', 'tooling gaps'), and how it differs from the literal project retro in the parent task. Implementation happens after plan is approved.

name: Split emailMagicLink into 3 context-specific templates
description:
Becky found a single 'magic link email' in Studio (current emailMagicLink singleton) — but the same template fires for 3 distinct contexts with different framing needs:

1. **Listen-page re-issue** (CheckEmailCard on /listen) — current copy fits: 'Open your reading'
2. **/my-readings sign-in** — copy should reference 'your readings library'
3. **/my-gifts sign-in** — copy should reference 'your gifts dashboard / scheduled deliveries'

Today the listen-flavored copy ships in all 3 contexts; /my-gifts purchasers get 'Open your reading' in their subject line.

Scope:
- studio/schemas/ — new singletons: emailMagicLinkListen, emailMagicLinkMyReadings, emailMagicLinkMyGifts (drop / rename emailMagicLink, decide naming with Becky)
- src/data/defaults.ts — 3 distinct EMAIL_MAGIC_LINK_*_DEFAULTS objects with context-appropriate copy

- Call sites: src/app/api/auth/magic-link/route.ts inspects the  URL parameter to derive context; pass through to sendMagicLink
- src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx (will be killed by task 2 anyway, but if shipped first: 3 new preview routes)

- Run seed against staging + production datasets
- Deprecate emailMagicLink singleton in Studio (leave doc in place; mark schema as @deprecated; remove from desk structure)
- Vitest cases: 3 call sites -> 3 different templates -> 3 different rendered HTML outputs

Acceptance:
- Becky sees 3 distinct email docs in Studio's ✉️ Emails group, each labeled with its surface
- /listen re-issue email reads correctly for listen context
- /my-gifts sign-in email reads as gifts (not 'open your reading')
- /my-readings sign-in email reads as readings library

Effort: medium (~3-4h). Mechanical schema + render split + careful call-site routing.

name: Harden GDPR export per-order throttle against TOCTOU race
description: Security review of P4 (release/v1.16.0, commit 3b86990) found a TOCTOU race in /api/privacy/export: recentExportTimestamp is read before the export_request audit row is written after the work, so concurrent requests with the same valid token can each build a ZIP + R2 object + email (bounded by the CF IP limiter LISTEN_AUTH_SEND_LIMITER). Impact is non-exfiltrating (emails always go to the order owner's registered email) but can redundantly email-bomb the owner. Fix: write the throttle/export_request row before the expensive ZIP/R2/email work, or dedupe via an atomic insert. Also consider a jti replay store if 1-year reusable bearer tokens become a concern. Not a launch blocker.
result: Export throttle row reserved before ZIP/R2/email, shrinking TOCTOU window (55ad9a5). Atomic jti store deferred to follow-up.

name: Listen audio: first-load parallel-request storm trips rate limiter + memory limit
description: Smoke 2026-05-27 (staging, submission 4b3b2bdb): on /listen load the browser opened ~20 parallel GET /api/listen/[id]/audio connections, ALL Canceled at the same instant (19:33:23). Symptoms: (1) 42x 'Error: Worker exceeded memory limit' (4.27MB voice file x ~20 concurrent streaming proxies approaches the 128MB cap), (2) LISTEN_ASSET_LIMITER fired -> user saw 'too many requests' 429, (3) audio recovered and played after the storm; PDF 200. Upstream cdn.sanity.io DOES honor Range (206 + content-range verified), but worker logged 200 on every audio request -> Range likely not forwarded/honored through the proxy, so each connection pulls toward the full file. Investigate client-side cause of the parallel burst (audio element preload + React effect/StrictMode double-mount?) AND why proxy returns 200 not 206. Primary audience is mobile/TikTok 375px where recovery is less certain. Files: src/app/api/listen/[id]/audio/route.ts, proxySanityAsset.ts (gateListenAssetRequest rate limit + forwardRangeHeader), listen page client audio component.
result: Fixed via PR #209 (squash 8357c32). Root cause: <audio preload=metadata> probed media on page load (server component, not React) -> no-Range full-file 200 streams -> ~20 concurrent x 4.27MB hit 128MB workerd cap -> stall/retry cascade -> 60/60s LISTEN_ASSET_LIMITER tripped -> 429. Fix: preload=none (zero load-time requests; play issues sequential Range requests). Proxy/route/rate-limit unchanged. Verified in real Chromium: metadata=1 req on load, none=0 on load, play=1 req Range bytes=0-.

name: Atomic dedupe store for /api/privacy/export (replace throttle-row reservation)
description: ojkl12it shipped a reserve-before-work mitigation that only shrinks the TOCTOU window (SELECT then INSERT are non-atomic). Replace with an atomic guard: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on a unique (submission_id, event_type, time-bucket) index, or a jti replay store. Separate the concurrency lock from the audit log (export_request is overloaded as both) and reserve on a distinct in-flight marker so a failed ZIP/R2/email doesn't 5-min-lock a legit retry. name: Replace in-spec mintListenToken with auth-gated /api/internal/test-mint-token endpoint
description: tests/e2e/specs/listen-one-tap-roundtrip.spec.ts mints tokens in-process via direct import of mintListenToken, which forces the CI env to carry STAGING_LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET (same value as staging worker's LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET) so token sigs verify on staging. This couples the spec to a secret-sync requirement, with rotation foot-guns (rotating staging secret nukes any in-flight giftClaimSession cookies since they share LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET — feedback_no_more_splitting_and_deferring notwithstanding). Better pattern: add POST /api/internal/test-mint-token route, auth-gated by STAGING_ADMIN_API_KEY (already in CI env). Spec calls the endpoint with submissionId + recipientUserId + mintSource + optional ttlMs and gets back a token. Spec no longer imports mintListenToken; no secret sync needed; rotation of LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET only invalidates outstanding tokens, doesn't break the test infrastructure. Implementation: ~30-line POST route + spec refactor that replaces direct mintListenToken calls with mintTokenForTest helper. Drop STAGING_LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET from e2e-sandbox.yml when done. Low risk; pure additive. Filed 2026-05-26 after the Phase 1 ship surfaced that the GH secret was never set and the auto-probe approach exposed the spec failure mode cleanly.

description: GOAL: collapse the 2-email day-7 delivery + magic-link chain into one tokenized one-tap email. Customer taps the button in the day-7 email and is signed in for 7 days; no form, no second email. SCOPE: (a) mint a 30-day HMAC-signed listen token in the day-7-deliver cron at src/app/api/cron/email-day-7-deliver/route.ts:38, embed in URL as ?t=<sig>; (b) extend /listen/[id] page at src/app/listen/[id]/page.tsx to read ?t= token, validate HMAC against LISTEN_TOKEN_SECRET, set the 7-day session cookie on first tap; (c) align admin manual-resend path at src/lib/booking/resendCustomerEmail.ts:94 to also mint a token via the same primitive (today it passes raw R2 URL, two-send-path divergence from audit); (d) preserve existing email-form sign-in as fallback for token-missing OR token-expired cases. ACCEPTANCE: cron-sent emails are one-tap; admin-resent emails use same URL semantics; expired tokens fall through to existing form; existing magic-link emails (EMAIL_MAGIC_LINK_*) continue to work unchanged for the no-token path. TEST PLAN: unit (token mint + validate); integration (cron path mints + page redeems); E2E sandbox (deliver, tap, listening). DEPENDS ON: nothing. SUPERSEDES dex 558hhza8 + m259wtc9. Memory: feedback_no_em_dashes (copy rewrite must drop em-dashes from defaults.ts:843, :859). Council ref: COUNCIL-1-chain-length.md unanimous recommendation #1.

name: GiftStatusPill SSR/client hydration mismatch on scheduled-at date
description: src/components/GiftStatusPill/GiftStatusPill.tsx:42-44 — formatScheduledMoment uses date-fns format() which renders in the runtime's local TZ. Server (Node, CI=UTC) and client (Playwright Chromium pinned to America/Los_Angeles in playwright.config.ts:59; real browsers in user TZ) disagree → 'Hydration failed because the server rendered text didn't match the client' fires on every /my-readings render with a scheduled gift. Currently non-breaking (React regenerates client-side, button selectors don't depend on pill text), but the console error is noisy in dev + Sentry, and it triggers a full client-side re-render of the gift card subtree. Fix: render the timestamp on the client only (useEffect-set state + suppressHydrationWarning, or a 'use client' wrapper that defers formatting to post-mount) OR pin both sides to a stable TZ string with format-tz. Surfaced 2026-05-30 during PR #218 e2e backfill (see also feedback_run_tests_locally_before_push).
result: GiftStatusPill defers date format to client via useIsClient hook (react-aria pattern); shipped via PR #230

name: Hero entrance animation blanks below-the-menu content on every reload (perceived load)
description:
SYMPTOM (Max, 2026-07-06, LIVE site): on every full reload — mobile Safari + Chrome AND desktop — everything below the menu is blank for a beat before appearing. Not the old RSC/render-latency issue (that was soft-nav, fixed by Path A).

ROOT CAUSE (confirmed from code, not browser-measured yet): src/components/Hero/Hero.tsx is a 'use client' component using framer-motion (motion/react). Every element below the logo has initial={{opacity:0, y/scale}} + animate with STAGGERED DELAYS up to 1.8s (tagline delay 0.3s, intro 0.75s, CTA 1.0s, scroll-cue 1.8s). framer renders the initial (opacity:0) state during SSR (visible as opacity:0 in the served HTML), so the content — though present in the HTML (Soul Blueprint/Birth Chart/Book a Reading all in the server payload) — stays INVISIBLE until the JS bundle downloads + hydrates, THEN the delayed entrance animation plays. On mobile, hydration latency compounds it. Hero is min-h-svh (full viewport) so it IS the 'below the menu' area on first paint.

NOT part of the problem: ReadingCard.tsx + FaqSection.tsx use AnimatePresence initial={false} — that's the accordion/expand INTERACTION (height animation on click), not an entrance reveal; they don't hide content on load. Scope the fix to Hero.tsx (and check SectionHeading/about+readings sections for any mount-time initial-hidden motion, though those are below fold).

FIX DIRECTIONS (decide aesthetic with Max — it's brand-critical 'quiet archivist' reveal):
(a) BEST perceived-perf: convert the hero entrance from JS/framer (post-hydration) to a pure CSS keyframe animation so the reveal starts at FIRST PAINT with no hydration wait; keep the elegant fade/slide but cap total reveal well under ~600ms.
(b) Or drop the entrance animation on the above-the-fold hero entirely (render visible), keep only interaction animations.
(c) Or minimal: cut the stagger delays (0.3–1.8s -> ~0) + shorten durations so it's a quick subtle fade, not a 2s staggered reveal.
Also: respect prefers-reduced-motion; the logo Image is priority (LCP) — hiding hero text via opacity:0 hurts LCP/CLS perception. Verify the fix with a real-browser (Playwright) timing check on the deployed site (real-browser-smoke), and note this is a UX/brand decision (loop Max / Designer).
result: Fixed via PR #323 (squash 8edc3fa, merged 2026-07-06): hero entrance moved from framer-motion (hydration-gated, SSR opacity:0, stagger to 1.8s) to CSS keyframes starting at first paint, settle <=600ms, prefers-reduced-motion = no animation. Direction (a) chosen by Max. Verified pre-merge: Playwright JS-disabled visible @375px, reduced-motion animationName none, 1821 tests, / static. Deployed-apex re-probe after prod deploy.

name: Email preview blank in Studio — needs proper diagnosis
description:
BLOCKER for v1.2.1 → main merge. Preview tab shows blank iframe on every email singleton in deployed Sanity Studio at https://withjosephine.sanity.studio/staging and /production.

==== STATE AT SESSION END 2026-05-24 ====

PR #187 (race-condition seq-guard) was CLOSED unmerged. Max reverted the local studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx back to the original cancellation-flag pattern (post-#181 state) — race-condition theory rejected.

PR #186 (strip render-blocking link from rendered HTML) is MERGED on release/v1.2.1. Deployed Studio includes the strip. Whether to keep #186 or revert it is part of next-session decision — strip might still be necessary even if not sufficient.

Deployed Studio bundle at session end has: strip-applied render-preview.tsx + cancellation-flag EmailPreview.tsx (post-revert). Max still sees blank.

==== COMMITS THAT TOUCH THIS SURFACE ====

- 950485f (#181): email preview pivot — moved render from /api/email-preview worker route to Studio iframe client-side. Max claims 'was working before' but visual verification post-merge never logged; treat as unverified.
- f8b8ee3 (#182): PT bodies — converted 7 email schemas string fields to PortableText array of block. EmailRichText union (string | string[] | PortableTextBlock[]). New PortableTextInline. Migration script NOT run pre-merge — caused validation errors Max saw later.
- 2a6e831 (#183): MagicLink split into 3 context singletons.
- 75f132b (#184): 90d retention + Day7 accessWindowLine field.
- 3764bc4 (#185): /preview/* worker routes (broken on staging due to CF Access — separate blocker).
- cc4b035 (#186, MERGED): strip render-blocking link. Deployed bundle has strip code (curl-verified).
- 194872a: session-boot carry-over commit.

==== EVIDENCE COLLECTED ====

LOCAL Playwright on Next.js dev server (debug page mirroring EmailPreview render call): PASSED. Status ok (4003 chars HTML), iframe body bbox 1406x672, visible text 'Hi Ada / Soul Blueprint / Open your reading'. renderEmailPreview itself returns valid renderable HTML when given valid input.

DEPLOYED bundle: curl https://withjosephine.sanity.studio/static/sanity-5Snnw2Yp.js | grep -c stripRenderBlockers → 2 matches. Strip IS in deployed code.

Max's 4 browser log files in ~/Downloads/ (timestamps 1779634527502, 1779635602918, 1779636076226, www.sanity.io-1779634527502): NO application errors. Only browser-extension noise, Sanity analytics CORS, WebSocket reconnects, vendor.js cssRules TypeError (Sanity noise). ZERO mentions of EmailPreview / renderEmailPreview / portabletext / blocking.

Deployed bundle hash: sanity-5Snnw2Yp.js. Local rebuild produced sanity--TYYnYpE.js (different — but both contain strip per grep).

==== NEXT SESSION INSTRUCTIONS ====

DO NOT ask Max to be the browser. Set up authenticated Playwright against deployed Studio yourself.

Auth approaches to try (fail fast):
1. ~/.config/sanity/config.json — check for stored auth token
2. ~/Library/Application Support/Google/Chrome/Default/Cookies — sqlite-read sanity.io cookies, copy into Playwright persistent context
3. SANITY_AUTH_TOKEN env if available
4. Fallback: pnpm sanity dev locally (no auth), reproduce EmailPreview consumption pattern at localhost:3333

Verify in authenticated session:
(1) Loaded sanity-XXX.js hash matches latest deploy. Divergent = CDN cache problem.
(2) Add temporary console.log in studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx for displayed._type / _id / html.length. Redeploy. Capture values.
(3) Iframe srcDoc attribute value — empty? has DOCTYPE? full HTML?
(4) Inside iframe: empty body / unstyled content / runtime error?

==== HYPOTHESES TO TEST IN ORDER ====

H1: Max's browser loads stale bundle from CDN cache despite deploy success. Test: note actual loaded sanity-XXX.js hash from Network tab, curl exact URL, grep for stripRenderBlockers. Absent = CDN cache problem.

H2: Sanity displayed doc shape mismatch after PT migration. Templates may throw on mixed (PT-from-sanity + string-from-defaults) merged shape. Test: console.log merged copy before render, compare to known-working.

H3: error state silently firing — error UI renders below fold or hidden. Test: scroll preview area, check for error card.

H4: ROLLBACK option. Revert studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx + src/lib/emails/render-preview.tsx to pre-#181 state (restore /api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx worker route), redeploy Studio. If THAT works, pivot direction was wrong, accept CSP carve-out.

H5: schema-data drift on drafts. Migration ran on published but Sanity may show DRAFT state. Drop drafts on a test singleton, republish, re-check.

==== ACTION CHECKLIST FOR NEXT SESSION ====

(1) Read this task with --full. Read sibling blocker tasks for gated-page replan, validation harness, cumulative simplify.
(2) DO NOT push fixes without authenticated-Playwright real-browser smoke against deployed Studio. Per feedback_stop_the_fix_spiral memory.
(3) Test H1 to H5 in order. Each yields concrete evidence; report once before deciding next step.
(4) If H4 becomes the answer: discuss with Max before reverting any merged PR.
(5) Bisect: if hypothesis points at a specific commit in #182-#185, git checkout that commit, redeploy Studio, verify.

Cannot ship release/v1.2.1 → main until preview is fixed AND Max can use it.
result:
Root cause: Chromium iframe srcdoc no-renavigate quirk. When EmailPreview's iframe loaded with srcDoc='' first and React later updated srcDoc to the 5921-char rendered HTML, Chrome correctly updated the attribute but did NOT re-navigate the iframe document. Body stayed empty.

Fix: 1-line addition to studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx — key={html ? 'loaded' : 'empty'} on the iframe. When html transitions null→string, React unmounts the empty iframe and mounts a fresh one with the real srcDoc from the start.

Diagnostic path that locked it:
- H7 refuted: PR #186 strip is a no-op for @react-email/render v2.0.8 in Node (still keeps the strip as harmless future-proofing)
- H6 refuted: sandbox='' renders correctly in standalone Chromium across all configs
- H8 refuted: faithful Sanity panel chain renders bare iframe correctly
- Deploy/source desync confirmed AND patched: deploy was running PR #187 seq-guard pattern that Max had closed locally
- Console instrumentation showed setHtml WAS being called with valid 5921-char HTML, cancelled=false, multiple times — but iframe body stayed empty
- DevTools probe in Studio app-frame context: iframe.srcdoc.length=5921 but inner body.length=0 → Chrome no-renavigate confirmed

Saved as durable memory: feedback_chromium_iframe_srcdoc_no_renavigate.md (project memory). Updated MEMORY.md index.

name: Speed up sandbox e2e (~14min today, target <8min)
description:
Sandbox suite runs 29 tests in ~14 min today against staging.withjosephine.com. Surfaced 2026-05-28 when Phase 4 PR #214 spec fixes turned 3 fast-fails into slow-passes, pushing suite past 10-min globalTimeout (bumped to 25min in 93f67a4).

DOMINANT COST: playwright.config.ts:36 sets workers: 1 with comment 'load-bearing fix for stability'. Pure serial execution. Halving requires bumping to workers: 2 safely.

INVESTIGATIONS NEEDED:
1. Why workers: 1 is load-bearing — original commit + breakage. Likely D1 row collisions on parallel paid-submission creation OR Sanity mirror eventual-consistency timing. Find the prior failure mode + fix the contention before bumping workers.
2. Per-spec randomization audit: ensure every spec creates submissions/users with random suffix so parallel runs don't collide. Most already do via randomUUID().slice(0, 8), but spot-check.
3. Sanity mirror sequencing under parallel load: writes are async runMirror; reads may race. Identify any spec that depends on mirror-after-write read ordering.

SECONDARY WINS:
- Backdoor admin endpoint for 'mark submission as paid' to skip Stripe Checkout in specs that don't test the Stripe UI (~10-15s saved per spec × N specs).
- Audit pollUntilPaid({ timeoutMs: 45_000 }) callsites: if staging is fast, no-op; if slow, full 45s wasted. Lower the cap or shorten the interval.
- Cache CF Access bearer token across tests (one per worker instead of every request).

GOAL: <8min sandbox runtime with green stability. Trigger: any time after PR #214 ships.
result: playwright.config.ts workers: isSandbox ? 2 : 1. Sandbox CI dropped from 14m to 10m26s (~28% reduction). Did not hit the <8min goal; secondary wins (backdoor mark-paid endpoint, pollUntilPaid timeout reduction) deferred. PR #250 squash.

name: Storybook page-level stories — primary UX/UI review surface
description:
WHY (the win Max named, 2026-05-23):
"See the design and tweak it" is currently expensive — every iteration requires pnpm dev, manually navigating to the page, seeding the right state in D1/Sanity, then hot-reloading. For Becky/Max-driven UX review on pages like /listen, /book, /intake, /letter, /thank-you, /my-gifts, /my-readings, that loop is too slow to actually try variations.

Storybook with mocked Sanity data turns this into: open the story, edit the component file, see the change in <1s with deterministic mock data. Becky doesn't have to be in the loop to provide fixtures.

POSITIONING (locked 2026-05-23):
This is a GATE for page-level UX/UI review work, not launch-blocking on its own. Going forward, "review the UX of page X" defaults to "do it in Storybook with the mocked story for page X." If a page doesn't have a story yet, the first task in any UX-review for that page is to add the story.

Exceptions: the U3/U4/U5/U7/U8 launch-blocking UX punch list (Epic wz9t979j) ships through the live dev server because Storybook setup likely won't beat apex unpark.

SCOPE (locked 2026-05-23):
Pages first, with mocked Sanity data. Components that pages compose get stories implicitly as their pages story. A pure-component-library Phase 2 may come later if Becky wants a design-system catalog, but that's not the immediate win.

Target page set (initial):
- /book/[slug] (entry)
- /letter
- /intake
- /thank-you/[readingId]
- /listen/[id] (incl. all 4 states: cold-visit / check-email / rested / DeliveredSurface)
- /my-gifts (incl. send-now armed, cancel-scheduled armed, claimed, scheduled, paid)
- /my-readings
- /my-readings/[id]

OPEN QUESTIONS (research-gated):
- Storybook version + Next.js App Router + RSC support (9.x latest as of 2026)
- Tailwind v4 + @theme directive wiring into preview-head
- Sanity GROQ mocking strategy: MSW handlers in the browser context (works — workerd-isolate gotcha from CLAUDE.md doesn't apply here), OR Sanity-client mock at the module level, OR prop-level injection of fetched data
- Storybook MCP — confirm canonical one (Storybook team announced an MCP server in 2025), what it exposes, whether it's useful for AI-assisted story authoring
- Routing: how to mock useParams / useSearchParams / next/navigation under @storybook/nextjs
- RSC stories: support landed in 8.4; verify it actually works for our App Router patterns
- Hosting: local-only vs static export deployed somewhere (Cloudflare Pages? GH Pages? Chromatic?) vs visual-regression testing in CI
- Workerd dev-bindings: do they need to be stubbed for stories to load, or does Storybook avoid the worker context entirely?
- Build perf: how Storybook builds against this Next.js codebase + how much it slows the local dev loop

RESUME PATH: research subtask first → plan-discussion subtask reads the research + locks decisions → implementation subtasks get filed AFTER the plan-discussion lands. Implementation tasks are deliberately NOT pre-created — let the research decide what the right shape is.
