name: Research — Sanity live-preview alternatives for transactional emails (re-evaluate D-1)
description:
PURPOSE
Validate or replace Phase 7 PRD decision D-1 before resuming PR-A1 ship.
D-1 LOCKED IN: custom S.view.component(EmailPreview) tab on each email
singleton, rendering /api/email-preview/[type] in a sandboxed iframe
against draft content via previewDrafts perspective.

D-1 REJECTED: Presentation Tool + defineLocations ("emails aren't web
routes").
D-1 NOT CONSIDERED: Sanity Visual Editing, custom field-level UI input
components, community email-preview plugins, send-to-test-inbox pattern,
Storybook-as-preview hybrid.

DELIVERABLE
Written report at www/MEMORY/WORK/<slug>/RESEARCH.md (gitignored per repo
convention) with vendor-doc citations and a recommendation: either
"Phase 7 D-1 stays — resume PR-A1 as-is" OR "pivot to approach X — rework
PR-A1's preview surface".

TOPICS TO COVER

1. CURRENT D-1 — what's actually shipped on the paused branch
- Read: studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx,
src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx,
src/middleware.ts (CSP widening for frame-ancestors),
studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts
(.views([form, S.view.component(EmailPreview)])).
- Honest assessment: does it actually deliver "easily live preview" for Becky?
- Known gaps (PRD risks 1, 2): CSP frame-ancestors widening, draft auth via
Origin allowlist.

2. SANITY PRESENTATION TOOL — defineLocations applied to emails
- 2026 API state. Can a non-URL doc-type be "located" at
/api/email-preview/[type]?
- Click-to-edit overlays on email body — useful for Becky or visual noise?
- Compare DX (click-through-from-list vs in-document-tab).
- Cost: rewrite of the iframe surface vs swap of the resolver.

3. SANITY VISUAL EDITING (enableVisualEditing) — overlay model
- Same URL constraint as Presentation. Compatible with
/api/email-preview/[type]?
- HTML-overlay UX on an iframed React Email — does it work? Is the email
layout
compatible with the overlay positioning?

4. CUSTOM FIELD-LEVEL UI INPUT COMPONENTS
- Sanity v5 components.input override per-field. Show inline preview NEXT to
subject / body / etc. as Becky edits.
- Granular but more code surface. Editor sees per-field preview, not
whole-email.

5. COMMUNITY SANITY PLUGINS for email preview
- Survey sanity.io/plugins for anything email-specific (e.g. mailto-preview,
react-email-preview, transactional-email integrations).
- Active vs abandoned (last commit date matters).

6. SEND-TO-TEST-INBOX PATTERN
- Studio doc-action: "Send preview to <becky-email>". Real-world rendering
across Gmail / Apple Mail / Outlook.
- Tradeoffs: slowest loop (~10s per send), most accurate (catches
client-specific
rendering bugs).
- Could be ADDITIVE to whichever live-preview approach wins.

7. STORYBOOK-AS-EMAIL-PREVIEW (cross-pollination with the storybook epic)
- Stories for each email template with arg controls for slot values.
- Pro: editor changes content in Storybook UI, sees instant React-Email
render.
- Con: Storybook is for developers, not Sanity editors. Becky doesn't use
Storybook.
- Hybrid: keep Storybook for dev-side email-template iteration; D-1 (or alt)
for
editor-side Sanity-content preview.

8. OFFLINE RENDER-CLI WORKFLOW (existing scripts/render-email-previews.ts)
- Today's tool. Watch-mode + browser-refresh pattern would be cheap to extend.
- Editor-unfriendly (CLI), but zero new code surface.

9. CROSS-CLIENT RENDERING — the dirty secret
- React Email + iframed preview shows Chrome rendering, not
Gmail/Outlook/Apple Mail.
- Email clients strip CSS aggressively (no flexbox-gap, no custom properties
without
fallbacks, no @media in some clients).
- Should D-1 (or alt) integrate Litmus / Email-On-Acid / Resend's built-in
cross-client preview? Cost? Or does the send-to-test pattern fill this gap?

10. SECURITY DELTA vs CURRENT D-1
- D-1 ships CSP frame-ancestors widening + cross-package Studio→worker draft
fetch.
- Each alternative: what's its security surface? Better, worse, neutral?

11. DECISION RUBRIC + RECOMMENDATION
- Decision criteria: (a) editor UX for Becky (most important), (b) code
complexity,
(c) security surface, (d) accuracy of preview vs real email rendering, (e)
code-
already-written cost (D-1 is built; pivot = rewrite).
- Final verdict: stay with D-1, or pivot. If pivot, name the replacement.

CONSTRAINTS

- Treat docs / blog posts from 2024 and earlier with skepticism — Sanity v5 +
the
Presentation Tool stabilization landed late 2024 / early 2025.
- Read the paused branch's actual files (studio/views/EmailPreview.tsx,
src/app/api/email-preview/[type]/route.tsx, src/middleware.ts CSP changes,
studio/schemas/deskStructure.ts). Don't theorize about D-1 — read the
implementation.
- The branch is `feat/phase-7-email-sanity-cms`, commit `f6568e9`. Check it
out
read-only OR use `git show` to inspect.
- Do NOT propose a plan / new PRD. Output is research — Max + a follow-up
plan-discussion decides the path.

OUTPUT: written report. Lead each topic with the answer. Cite vendor docs and
the paused-branch files inline. End with the recommendation.

STARTING REFERENCE (sent by Max 2026-05-23):
https://www.sanity.io/docs/studio/create-custom-document-views-with-structure-builder

This is the canonical Sanity docs page for the approach D-1 currently uses
(custom document views via S.view.component). Ground topic 1 (current D-1
assessment) in this doc; cross-check against the actual implementation on
the paused branch. If the doc reveals capabilities D-1 isn't using (e.g.
multiple coordinated views, view-level routing, dynamic view loading),
flag them — they may be cheaper improvements than a wholesale pivot.

ADDITIONAL REFERENCE (sent by Max 2026-05-23):
https://github.com/sanity-io/plugins

Official Sanity plugins monorepo — canonical source for maintained-by-Sanity
plugins. Topic 5 (community Sanity plugins for email preview) MUST scan this
repo first (not just sanity.io/plugins search). For each candidate: last
commit date, maintenance posture, Sanity v5 compatibility, fit for our
email-preview use case. If a sanity-io/plugins/* entry covers the gap (e.g.
preview-kit, presentation-related helpers, dashboard widgets that could
host an email preview), call it out explicitly with verdict.
result: ENHANCE D-1, do NOT pivot. Recommendation backed by weighted decision matrix (editor UX, code cost, security, accuracy, sunk-cost). Three actions: (1) swap hand-rolled iframe for sanity-plugin-iframe-pane Iframe with reload.revision=true (~30min if reload.revision works as npmx changelog claims), (2) optional send-to-test doc action (~1d), (3) optional defineLocations additive layer (~1h). Skip Visual Editing (wrong shape for emails). Storybook + render-CLI stay dev-side. 4 open questions logged for plan-discussion: reload.revision validation, send-to-test recipient-allowlist source, defineLocations yes/no, cross-client tooling deferral.
